name: Farmers & Merchants Bank & Trust
description_short: Community bank serving the Greater Burlington, Iowa area since 1916, offering personal and business banking with local decision-making and FDIC insurance.
description_long: Farmers & Merchants Bank & Trust (F&M) was originally chartered on March 1, 1916, as Farmers & Merchants Savings Bank and became Farmers & Merchants Bank & Trust in 1965. For over a century, the institution has remained locally owned and operated in the Greater Burlington, Iowa area, maintaining a focus on community relationships and personal service. The bank emphasizes that decisions are made locally rather than at a corporate headquarters, allowing for faster response to community needs. F&M Bank & Trust offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, youth checking accounts, digital banking through mobile app and online platforms, business services with credit card payment processing (merchant services), trust services, brokerage services, and home lending products. The bank provides modern banking technology alongside traditional personal service, with multiple login types for personal, business, trust, and brokerage accounts. They also participate in Zelle for money transfers and offer financial calculators to help customers with financial planning. The bank distinguishes itself primarily through its commitment to local ownership and community investment. Marketing materials emphasize that mortgages stay local and that customer money "works harder" when banking locally. F&M positions itself as more than a bank but as a community partner, explicitly noting their investment in local families, businesses, schools, and organizations. The emphasis on "decisions made right here locally" is a core differentiator from larger regional or national banking institutions. As a legitimate FDIC-insured bank operating since 1916, F&M Bank & Trust is a stable financial institution. However, the website provides limited specific information about account features, interest rates, fees, or loan terms. The bank is geographically limited to the Greater Burlington, Iowa area, which may restrict accessibility for customers outside this region. Additionally, the website lacks detailed information about specific product offerings, minimum balances, or competitive rate comparisons that consumers typically need for banking decisions.
services: Merchant services and credit card payment processing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Over 108 years of continuous operation since original 1916 charter, demonstrating stability
Locally owned and operated with loan decisions made in the community rather than at distant corporate headquarters
Offers multiple account types including personal, business, trust, and brokerage services
Digital banking options including mobile app and online banking alongside traditional personal service
Participates in Zelle for fast and safe money transfers to other banks
Merchant services available for business customers to manage credit card payments
cons: Geographic limitation to Greater Burlington, Iowa area reduces accessibility for non-local customers
Website provides minimal specific information about account features, interest rates, fees, or loan terms needed for comparison shopping
Limited online information about specific product offerings, minimum balance requirements, or competitive rate details
No mention of ATM network access or surcharge-free ATM partnerships
Website lacks detail on business loan products, lines of credit, or specific lending terms
best_for: Residents and business owners in the Greater Burlington, Iowa area seeking community-focused banking with local decision-making
Customers who prioritize relationship banking and personal service over large national banks
Small business owners needing merchant services and local credit decisions
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured savings and checking with digital banking convenience
diagnosis: F&M Bank & Trust is best for residents and business owners in the Greater Burlington, Iowa area who value community banking, local decision-making, and personal service over large national institutions. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank serves only the Greater Burlington region, making it inaccessible for customers outside Southeast Iowa, and the website lacks sufficient detail about specific rates, fees, and product features needed for comprehensive comparison with competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
